headline: Perak Limestone Hills Quarrying Destroys 12 Endemic Species Habitats
context: A 2025 MNS biodiversity survey found that 12 species endemic to Perak's limestone hills have lost more than 80% of their habitat due to quarrying expansion since 2010.

cards.big: Perak's limestone hills host species found nowhere else on Earth.
cards.sub: 12 endemic species — including 4 snails, 3 plants, and 2 geckos — have lost over 80% of their habitat since 2010.

cards.big: Limestone quarrying in Perak is worth RM420M annually.
cards.sub: The entire tourism value of Perak's karst landscapes — including Gua Tempurung — is RM180M.

cards.big: Malaysia has no specific legislation protecting limestone habitats.
cards.sub: The Wildlife Conservation Act 2010 protects species but not the habitats they depend on.

cards.big: Vietnam declared 8 limestone karst areas as national heritage in 2020.
cards.sub: Malaysia has not gazette any limestone hills as protected areas at federal level.

cards.big: Protecting a species while quarrying its only habitat is biological nonsense.
Habitat protection legislation is the missing piece in Malaysia's biodiversity framework. Species-only protection is like insuring a house but not the land it stands on.
